original: Usain Boltof Jamaica wraps up the 100/200 m double in style with a new 200 m world record of 19.30 seconds at the Beijing Olympics
NASA confirms a mission to Jupiter's ice-covered moon Europa to search for alien life, launching in 2025
NASA launches Voyager 2 towards Jupiter and Saturn, later reaching Uranus and Neptune
"Sailor Moon" by Naoko Takeuchi, one of the most popular manga of all time, is first published in Japan
Miracle the white buffalo, an extremely rare white, non-albino buffalo calf, born in Janesville Wisconsin
Viking 1 is launched to orbit around Mars and performs a soft landing
Chinese diver Chen Aisen achieves the Olympic 10 m platform double in Rio de Janeiro; having already won the synchronized event, he wins the individual gold by a comfortable margin
Pittsburgh Pirate Omar Moreno steals a record 70 bases for the third consecutive season

Battle of Thermopylae: In one of history's most famous last stands, 7,000 warriors from an alliance of Greek states led by Leonidas I block the pass of Thermopylae for a week against an invading Persian army under Xerxes I, estimated at 120,000 to 300,000 strong [date is approximate]
Planets Venus and Jupiter in conjunction - possible astronomical explanation for the Star of Bethlehem
First pilot to parachute from an aircraft (Adolphe Pégoud, France)

Soviet satellite Korabl-Sputnik 2 (Sputnik 5) returns to Earth with its passengers: two dogs, Belka and Strelka, two mice, two rats, flies, several plants, and fungi, the first Earth-born creatures to go into orbit and return alive
American swimmerMichael Phelpswraps up the 100/200 m butterfly double at the Athens Olympics when he wins the 100 m (51.25 OR) ahead of teammate Ian Crocker
FIFA Women's World Cup Final, Stadium Australia, Sydney: Spanish captain Olga Carmona scores the only goal of the game as La Furia Roja score a 1-0 win over England

PGA Championship Men's Golf, Valhalla GC:Tiger Woodswins back-to-back PGA titles after a three-hole playoff with Bob May, becoming the first to win three majors in a calendar year sinceBen Hoganin 1953
N. K. Jemisin is the first African American author to win the Hugo Award for Best Novel with her work "The Fifth Season"
Ke Huy Quan [Jonathan Ke Quan], Vietnamese-American actor (Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom, Everything, Everywhere All at Once), born in Saigon, South Vietnam
Seventeen-year-old Dodgers utility player Tommy Brown becomes the youngest player to hit an MLB home run in Brooklyn's 11-1 rout of the Pittsburgh Pirates at Ebbets Field
First airship flight around Earth flying eastward is completed
"Black Saturday" of the Yellowstone fire in Yellowstone National Park, Wyoming
Russia's Luna 25 spacecraft crashes into the Moon, according to Roscosmos, the country's space agency
American Professional Football Association forms withJim Thorpeas president and later becomes the National Football League (NFL)
Ursula K. Le Guin is the first woman to win the Hugo Award for Best Novel with her work "The Left Hand of Darkness"
American Janet Evans swims the women's 800 m freestyle world record of 8:16.22 at the Pan Pacific Championships in Tokyo, Japan
Norbert Rózsa swims a world record in the 100 m breaststroke (1:01.29)
Russian long-distance swimmer Larisa Ilchenko wins inaugural women's open-water marathon (10 km) in 1:59:27.7 at the Beijing Olympics
Cătălina Ponor, Romanian gymnast (3 Olympic gold 2004), born in Constanța, Romania
Tetsuzo Akutsu, Japanese surgeon who built the first artificial heart, born in Japan (d. 2007)
109.8°F (43.2°C) is recorded in Cordoba, Spain
69 cm of rainfall in Nelson County, Virginia (state record)
USAF balloon, manned by David Simons, breaks an altitude record at 102,000 ft (31,089.6 m) over Minnesota and South Dakota
First Women's World Games (first track and field competition for women) are conducted over one day at Pershing Stadium in Paris
In an epic first-round US men's singles tennis match, Palmer Presbrey takes 80 games to defeat T.S. Tailer, 19-21, 8-6, 6-1, 3-6, 6-4
Las Lajas Sanctuary, a basilica built inside the Guáitara River Canyon in Colombia, is completed after 33 years
Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky's "1812 Overture" debuts in Moscow
David Walliams, English actor, comedian (Come Fly With Me) and children's author, born in London, England
Ben Barnes, English actor (Prince Caspian-The Chronicles of Narnia), born in London
Hideki Shirakawa, Japanese chemist (Nobel Prize 2000 - discovery and development of conductive polymers), born in Tokyo

Eero Saarinen, Finnish-American architect (Dulles Airport (Virginia); Gateway Arch (St. Louis, Missouri); IBM Building (Rochester, Minnesota); MIT Chapel (Cambridge, Massachusetts)), born in Kirkkonummi, Finland (d. 1961)[1]
NY Yankees future Baseball Hall of Fame first basemanLou Gehrighits his record 23rd and final grand slam in an 11-3 win over the Philadelphia A's at Shibe Park
MLB New York Mets infielderHoward Johnsonjoins Barry Bonds andWillie Maysby hitting 30 home runs and stealing 30 bases in a season
England scores a world ODI record of 363 runs in 55 overs against Pakistan
Ecuador votes in a referendum against drilling for oil in Yasuní National Park, a world biosphere reserve inhabited by Indigenous groups in the Amazon
Tropical Storm Hilary makes landfall on Mexico's Baja Peninsula, the first tropical storm to hit the region in 84 years
New York Yankees player Phil Linz plays harmonica on bus despite managerYogi Berra's orders
America's Great White Fleet arrives in Sydney, Australia, greeted with a tremendous welcome; 221 American sailors desert to stay in Australia
Rudolf Virchow's groundbreaking medical text "Cellular Pathology" is published in Berlin, establishing the basis for modern medical science
Francis Drakerenames his flagship the "Pelican" to the "Golden Hind" in honor of his patron, Sir Christopher Hatton
Battle of Fallen Timbers: Major General "Mad Anthony" Wayne defeats a joint British-Native American force at Fallen Timbers, Ohio in the final battle of the Northwest Indian War
British Prime Minister Churchill says of the Royal Air Force, "Never in the field of human conflict was so much owed by so many to so few"
Second Salon exhibition by the Society of Independent Artists is held in Paris and includesGeorges Seurat's "A Sunday Afternoon on the Island of La Grande Jatte"
Chinese revolutionarySun Yat-senforms the first chapter of Tongmenghui, a union of secret societies determined to bring down the Qing dynasty
Bernardo O'Higgins, Chilean independence leader who freed Chile from Spanish rule (Supreme Director of Chile 1817-23), born in Chillán, Captaincy General of Chile (d. 1842)
Jacopo Peri, Italian composer and singer who wrote the 1st recognized opera "Dafne", born in Rome, Papal States (d. 1633)
Dulcie September, South African teacher and anti-apartheid political activist, born in Athlone, Western Cape, South Africa (d. 1988)
Ralf Hütter, German electronic music keyboardist and singer (Kraftwerk), born in Krefeld, Germany
James Pankow, American trombonist, arranger and composer (Chicago - "Make Me Smile"; "Colour My World"), born in St. Louis, Missouri
Phil Lynott, Irish rock singer and bass player (Thin Lizzy - "The Boys Are Back In Town"), born in West Bromwich, England. (d. 1986)
Greg Bear, American sci-fi author (2 Hugos, Eon, Eternity), born in San Diego, California (d. 2022)
Greg Egan, Australian science fiction and hard science author, born in Perth, Australia
